description: Pattern catalogue for the safe-package-change PreToolUse hook. Maps 1:1 to PLAN.md §3 threat-model addendum (M1.6, PR #18). Defence in depth with the per-project .npmrc that /setup-project writes (ignore-scripts=true, minimum-release-age=10080, audit-level=moderate from PLAN.md §4) — npmrc is the silent baseline; this hook surfaces explicit decisions and catches the cases where the operator (or a transitive dep) deliberately re-enables what npmrc forbids.

patterns.name: non-ascii-package-name
patterns.description: Package name contains non-ASCII characters (homoglyph attack)
patterns.pattern_note: Negated range of printable ASCII (0x20–0x7E). Equivalent to [^\x00-\x7F] modulo control characters, which are not valid in npm package names anyway. We use the printable form because BSD grep on macOS does not expand \xNN.
patterns.rationale: Homoglyph-attack defence — `lodash` and `lоdash` (Cyrillic о) look identical but are different packages. Never install a non-ASCII-named package without explicit operator review.

patterns.name: typosquatting-suspect
patterns.description: Package name within Levenshtein distance ≤ 2 of a well-known package (but not an exact match)
patterns.rationale: Looks one or two character edits away from a popular package. Could be a deliberate brand-adjacent fork, could be a typosquat. Operator decides.

patterns.name: package-age-under-7-days
patterns.description: Direct dependency younger than 7 days (first publish to npm)
patterns.rationale: PLAN.md §4: never install a package younger than 7 days. Mirrors the per-project .npmrc minimum-release-age guardrail at the runtime layer.

patterns.name: lifecycle-script-fetch-execute
patterns.description: preinstall/postinstall/prepare/install script that fetches and executes remote code
patterns.rationale: Lifecycle script downloads and executes remote code at install time — the textbook supply-chain-attack shape. Block unconditionally.

patterns.name: lifecycle-script-added
patterns.description: Adding/changing a preinstall/postinstall/prepare/install lifecycle script (any content)
patterns.rationale: Any lifecycle script runs code at install time. Allowlisted native-build packages (sharp, puppeteer, playwright, node-gyp, etc.) get a pass; everything else requires operator confirmation.

patterns.name: bin-path-traversal
patterns.description: `bin` field value contains `../` or starts with `/`
patterns.rationale: A bin entry escaping the package directory can plant an arbitrary executable on PATH at install time.

patterns.name: non-registry-version-specifier
patterns.description: Dependency declared with a git URL, tarball URL, or `file:` path (anything not `https://registry.npmjs.org`)
patterns.rationale: Bypasses npm's audit, integrity check, and minimum-release-age. Valid for genuine private forks, but the operator should confirm intent.

patterns.name: lockfile-removal
patterns.rationale: Removing the lockfile means the next install resolves fresh, defeating reproducibility and audit. Use `pnpm install` to update intentionally.
